sketch-what-is-sql-env.title: What Is a SQL Practice Environment?
sketch-what-is-sql-env.bodyMarkdown: A SQL practice environment is a place where you can write and run SQL commands safely. You can set up a local environment on your computer or use an online tool. Practicing in a dedicated environment helps you learn SQL without affecting real data.

sketch-local-vs-online.title: Local vs. Online SQL Environments
sketch-local-vs-online.bodyMarkdown: You can practice SQL by installing a database like SQLite or MySQL on your computer (local), or by using online platforms such as DB Fiddle, SQL Fiddle, or SQLite Online. Online tools are quick to start and require no installation, while local setups give you more control and flexibility.

sketch-first-sql-command.title: Running Your First SQL Command
sketch-first-sql-command.bodyMarkdown: Once your environment is ready, try running a simple SQL command like `SELECT 1;`. This checks that your setup works and helps you get comfortable with the SQL interface.

quiz-local-vs-online.title: Which of the following is an advantage of using an online SQL environment?
quiz-local-vs-online.prompt: Which of the following is an advantage of using an online SQL environment?

options.a: You can start practicing immediately without installing anything.
options.b: You have full control over server configuration.
options.c: You can access your environment without an internet connection.
options.d: You must pay to use online SQL tools.

quiz-first-command.title: What does the SQL command `SELECT 1;` do?
quiz-first-command.prompt: What does the SQL command `SELECT 1;` do?

options.a: It selects all data from table 1.
options.b: It returns the number 1 as a result.
options.c: It creates a new table called 1.
options.d: It deletes row number 1 from a table.

quiz-setup-step-order.title: Put these steps in order to set up an online SQL environment.
quiz-setup-step-order.prompt: Put these steps in order to set up an online SQL environment.

tokens.t1: Open an online SQL tool in your browser
tokens.t3: Click 'Run' or 'Execute' to see the result

quiz-sql-practice-code.title: Practice: Run a simple SQL command to test your environment.
quiz-sql-practice-code.prompt: Write a SQL statement that returns the number 5.
